combat_history.event: First major operational deployment with full air wing, conducted flight operations near disputed Spratly Islands
combat_history.significance: Demonstrated China's commitment to projecting power in contested waters

combat_history.event: Sailed through Taiwan Strait during heightened tensions, accompanied by destroyer escort
combat_history.significance: Political signaling during period of increased cross-strait tensions

combat_history.event: Extended deployment east of first island chain with multiple J-15 flight operations
combat_history.significance: First major blue-water deployment showing growing operational confidence

modernization.description: Upgrading combat data systems and sensor integration, improved datalinks with escort vessels
modernization.impact: Better coordination with task group elements and improved situational awareness

modernization.description: Adding more J-15 variants including electronic warfare and tanker versions
modernization.impact: Enhanced air wing capabilities and operational flexibility

vulnerabilities.description: Ski-jump launch restricts aircraft payload and fuel loads compared to catapult systems
vulnerabilities.context: Limits strike range and heavy payload missions, reducing offensive capability against distant targets
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Future Type 003 carrier will have electromagnetic catapults

vulnerabilities.description: Relatively small air wing (24-28 fighters) compared to US carriers (60+ aircraft)
vulnerabilities.context: Limited sustained air operations and reduced ability to maintain combat air patrol while conducting strikes
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Operational doctrine emphasizes quality over quantity and coordination with land-based aviation

vulnerabilities.description: PLAN has limited carrier operational experience and doctrine development compared to US Navy
vulnerabilities.context: Potential for tactical and operational mistakes during high-intensity operations
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Extensive training programs and gradual operational expansion to build expertise

vulnerabilities.description: Steam propulsion limits operational flexibility and requires more frequent refueling
vulnerabilities.context: Reduces deployment duration and operational tempo compared to nuclear-powered carriers
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Adequate for regional operations within Chinese strategic priorities

operational_patterns.typical_deployment: Carrier strike group operations in South China Sea and Western Pacific
operational_patterns.typical_task_group: 2-3 Type 052D destroyers, 1-2 Type 054A frigates, Type 901 supply ship
operational_patterns.readiness_notes: High operational tempo for training, increasing operational deployments

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Shandong is larger with greater aircraft capacity but both use ski-jump launch. Similar displacement but different strategic focus areas.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Asian carrier competition - comparing China and India's indigenous carrier capabilities

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Shandong incorporates Chinese improvements to basic Kuznetsov design with better electronics and reliability, but maintains same basic limitations.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Evolution of Soviet carrier design - how China improved on Russian blueprint

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Queen Elizabeth is larger, more modern design with better aviation facilities, but Shandong has more defensive armament and operates in more contested environment.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Ski-jump carriers: British innovation vs Chinese adaptation

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Ford-class represents gold standard with electromagnetic catapults, nuclear power, and much larger air wing. Shandong is stepping stone toward similar capability.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Supercarrier gap - how close is China to matching US carrier capability

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Kaga is much smaller helicopter carrier being modified for F-35B, while Shandong is full-size conventional carrier with larger air wing but less advanced aircraft.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Pacific carrier race - Japan's F-35B ships vs China's conventional carriers

doctrine.role: Regional power projection and sea control within the second island chain, serving as the centerpiece of carrier battle groups designed to challenge US naval dominance in the Western Pacific while providing expeditionary capabilities for Chinese interests beyond traditional coastal defense.
doctrine.employment: Operates as the nucleus of a carrier strike group alongside Type 055 destroyers, Type 052D destroyers, and Type 054A frigates, with Type 093/094 submarine escorts. Primary mission profiles include sea lane control in the South China Sea, Taiwan contingency operations, and power projection into the Indian Ocean. The carrier operates under theater command structure, integrating with land-based DF-21D and DF-26 anti-ship ballistic missiles for layered defense. Training cycles emphasize multi-domain operations with coordinated strikes involving carrier aviation, surface combatants, and shore-based missile forces.
doctrine.design_philosophy: Prioritized rapid indigenous construction capability and pilot training capacity over advanced catapult technology, accepting ski-jump limitations to accelerate operational experience. The design sacrificed aircraft payload and sortie generation rates for construction simplicity and reduced technical risk, reflecting China's focus on building naval aviation expertise rather than immediate technological parity with US carriers. Island modifications and improved flight deck layout demonstrate incremental improvement philosophy over revolutionary advancement.
doctrine.threat_context: Designed for contested environments where US submarine threats and long-range precision strikes necessitate integrated air defense and multi-layered protection from accompanying surface combatants. The threat environment has intensified since construction began, with increased US freedom of navigation operations, enhanced allied coordination through AUKUS and Quad partnerships, and accelerated US hypersonic weapon development forcing greater reliance on shore-based missile support.
doctrine.comparison_frame: Compare primarily on air wing capacity, sortie generation rates, and defensive integration rather than pure platform capabilities—PLAN doctrine emphasizes system-of-systems warfare over individual platform performance. Aircraft launch rates and operational availability matter more than displacement or speed, as Chinese carriers operate within land-based missile coverage unlike US carriers designed for independent power projection. Focus on training throughput and maintenance cycles, as China prioritizes building institutional knowledge over immediate combat capability.
